questions.text: I hold the view that it's okay to put my needs above the expectations of others.
When things go sideways, I look for a collective response rather than taking personal charge.
I think that my ability to remain composed when others are in distress is a quality that has made me a more reliable person.
I feel that building my personal brand is a smart investment of time.
I think that the capacity to remain logically oriented during emotionally difficult moments is what makes some people reliable in a crisis.
I feel that a raise or bonus is best used to help the people around me.
I see myself as someone who makes things happen.
I feel that I absorb the emotional tone of a room the moment I walk in.
I would rather be an integral part of a large group than lead a small one.
I notice that my sleep is rarely disrupted by emotional events from the day.
I feel more in control when I have a plan, even if it needs to be adjusted later.
I tend to figure things out as I go rather than reading instructions first.
I find that the moral pull of suffering is the experience that has most profoundly shaped my sense of obligation to the people and communities I belong to.
I do not usually pick up on whether someone is genuinely enthusiastic or just being polite.
Even a glancing disappointment registers in me as a significant blow.
I tend not to detect when a friendly question is actually a form of gatekeeping.
I learn best by jumping in and experimenting rather than following a structured path.
I experience the end of something meaningful with a sense of completion rather than devastation.
